title: Read OpenAI’s latest internal memo about beating the competition — including Anthropic
description: OpenAI's chief revenue officer, Denise Dresser, sent a four-page memo to employees on Sunday about the company's strategic direction, emphasizing the need to lock in users and grow its enterprise business. The memo, which was viewed by The Verge, repeatedly underlines the importance of building a moat around its AI products, to combat how easy […]
summary:
Here's a 2-3 sentence summary for a news show segment:

"OpenAI is taking aim at the competition with a new internal memo from their chief revenue officer. In it, she emphasizes the importance of 'locking in' users and growing their enterprise business - basically creating a strong fortress around their AI products to keep others out. It's a clear sign that OpenAI is gearing up for a battle in the rapidly evolving world of artificial intelligence."

title: Microsoft is testing OpenClaw-like AI bots for Copilot
description: Microsoft is looking into ways it can integrate OpenClaw-style features into its Copilot AI assistant, according to a report from The Information. The test reportedly comes as part of efforts to make Microsoft 365 Copilot "run autonomously around the clock" while completing tasks on behalf of users. Omar Shahine, Microsoft's corporate vice president, confirmed to […]
summary:
Here's a summary of the article in 3 conversational sentences:

Microsoft is taking a cue from OpenClaw, an AI bot that can tackle complex tasks on its own, and testing similar features for its Copilot assistant. The goal is to make Copilot even more autonomous, so it can run 24/7 and handle tasks like a personal assistant. If successful, this could bring new levels of productivity to Microsoft users - think automated emails, reports, and more, all handled by your AI sidekick!

title: The AI code wars are heating up
description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on the AI coding and vibe-coding booms, follow David Pierce. The Stepback arrives in our subscribers' inboxes at 8AM ET. Opt in for The Stepback here. How it started Writing code was a killer app for AI […]
summary:
Here's a summary of the article in 3 sentences:

"Welcome back to the tech world, where the battle for artificial intelligence supremacy is heating up! A new wave of "vibe-coding" and "AI coding" booms are sweeping the industry, with coders competing to create the most innovative algorithms. But as AI becomes more powerful, one thing's clear: the winners will be those who can write the code that sets them apart."
